item.title: Estival Jazz Lugano changes format: two paid evenings
item.description: From 2026, Estival Jazz offers two paid evenings with Ernia and Antonello Venditti. The era of free concerts is over.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Estival Jazz changes format: two paid evenings
- Ernia and Antonello Venditti are the first announced artists
- End of the era of free concerts

## Key facts
- **What**: Change of format for Estival Jazz
- When: From 2026
- Where: Lugano
- Who: City of Lugano, Roberto Badaracco, Jacky Marti
- Amount: Tickets from 57.75 CHF to 65 CHF

LUGANO - Times change and so do institutions; the powers that be can only adapt. This is the case for Estival, which for its 46th edition has decided, as already anticipated at the end of March, to change its formula: no longer (only) free concerts. But two paid dates with highly attractive names.

The Lugano festival, known for its tradition of absolute quality, has announced two paid evenings for 2026. The first artist, the trapper-singer Ernia, will perform on Saturday, July 11th in Piazza Riforma. The second, Antonello Venditti, will be the star of the evening on Friday, July 10th.

The decision was announced at a press conference with a sad and particularly subdued tone, dominated by long silences. Present were the Head of Department Roberto Badaracco, the Director of the Events and Congresses Division Claudio Chiapparino, and what is now the former artistic director of Estival, Jacky Marti, who confirmed his step back.

The reason for the change, as anticipated by the deputy mayor himself, is linked to costs and the need to 'find a new balance capable of reconciling artistic excellence and economic sustainability'.

### A golden age that will not return

'Both under the direction of Marti-Wyden and with the participation of the City of Lugano, Estival Jazz can boast a tradition of absolute quality, great visibility on a global level, and of which we are extremely proud,' adds Badaracco, '45 years that represent a golden age that, it is sad to say, frankly is no longer repeatable. Lugano has changed but so has the world. And it is important not to remain anchored to one's positions, but to have the courage to change'.

The 'break' of the Estival Jazz model comes in the post-Covid era with a gradual decrease in interest from sponsors, which has alerted the organizing committee, which has therefore directly consulted the City.

### Free access is no longer feasible

'One thing has been clear to us from the start: we did not want to lose Estival Jazz,' continues Badaracco, who reiterates that the path of free access 'is no longer feasible today, with artists asking for more and more, sponsors much less generous and more attentive to image return, and - lastly - the 10 million savings wanted by the City that in the last period have forced us to 'do somersaults'.

'It breaks our heart,' he adds, 'we know that many realities have been affected, and Estival is one of them'.

The deputy mayor responds to the criticisms that have appeared on the web, among social media and the like: 'We have always been an Eldorado a l ## Implications for cross-border workers

The change in Estival Jazz's format will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who traditionally attended the free concerts. With the introduction of paid tickets, the costs of attending the events will increase, affecting the participation of those who live in Italy and work in Ticino.

### Before/After Comparison

Before the change, Estival Jazz was known for its free concerts, which attracted a large and diverse audience. Now, with the introduction of paid tickets, the dynamics might change. Cross-border workers will have to evaluate whether the cost of the tickets is justified by the quality of the artists and the experience offered.

### Concrete Scenarios

A cross-border worker who regularly attended the free concerts might have to give up or reduce their frequency of attending events due to the additional costs. On the other hand, those willing to pay might enjoy a more exclusive experience with high-profile artists.

### Economic Impact

The introduction of paid tickets could also affect the local economy. Restaurants, bars, and shops that traditionally benefit from the flow of visitors during the concerts might see a reduction in turnover. However, the appeal of internationally renowned artists could compensate for this loss, attracting a wider audience willing to spend.

### Final Considerations

The change in Estival Jazz's format represents both a challenge and an opportunity. For cross-border workers, it will be important to carefully evaluate the costs and benefits of attending the events. For the city of Lugano, it will be crucial to find a balance between economic sustainability and artistic quality to maintain the festival's appeal.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers who wish to attend Estival Jazz events, it's important to plan ahead. Here are some steps to follow:

1. Check the dates and artists: Review the official Estival Jazz calendar to know the dates and artists scheduled.
2. **Buy tickets in advance**: Tickets are available on biglietteria.ch and prices range from 57.75 CHF to 65 CHF.
3. Plan your trip: If you reside in Italy, consider the time needed to reach Lugano and organize your trip accordingly.
4. Evaluate costs: Consider not only the ticket price, but also transportation, parking, and any expenses for food and drinks.
5. Take advantage of offers: Keep an eye out for any special offers or discounts for group ticket purchases.

### Useful Tools

To help you plan your participation in Estival Jazz events, you can use the following tools:

- Cost calculator: Use the [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate the total costs of your trip, including transportation, parking, and meals.
- Ticket comparator: Compare ticket prices on different platforms to find the best deals.
- Border map: Use the [border map](nav:border-map) to plan your trip and find the most convenient border crossings.

### Conclusion

The change in format of Estival Jazz represents a new challenge for cross-border workers, but with careful planning, it is possible to fully enjoy the events. Use the available tools to assess costs and organize your participation effectively.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921420/estival-lugano-badaracco-marti-jacky)*

item.title: Olgiate Olona Solidarity Emporium Opens
item.description: Inauguration on Saturday, May 9th with info stands and community aperitif. Local groups like Mensa del Padre Nostro and CAV of Castellanza will participate.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Inauguration of Emporio Solidarietà Olgiate Olona
- Participation of local entities
- Concluding community aperitif

## Key facts
- **What**: Inauguration of the Solidarity Emporium
- When: Saturday, May 9, 2026
- Where: Parish of San Giuseppe al Buon Gesù, Olgiate Olona
- Who: Decanato Valle Olona, Monsignor Franco Agnesi, volunteers
- Amount: Not specified

On Saturday, May 9, 2026, at 9:30 AM, the inauguration of the Solidarity Emporium will take place at the Parish of San Giuseppe al Buon Gesù in Olgiate Olona. The event, organized by the Decanato Valle Olona, will see the participation of numerous local entities engaged in supporting vulnerabilities. Among these are the Mensa del Padre Nostro and the CAV of Castellanza, the Amicizia Group of Gorla Minore, Icore of Marnate, Il pane di San Martino of Fagnano and Gorla Maggiore. For Olgiate Olona, the communities Sichem and Pachamama with the cooperative Promozione e Lavoro will be present, supported by Solidarietà Famigliare, In cammino per la Pace, and the Ambrosian Caritas decanale.

The morning will begin with informational stands, followed at 10 AM by the presentation of the project together with the volunteers of the Emporium of Saronno and the intervention of Monsignor Franco Agnesi, Vicar General of the Diocese. At 12 PM, the official ribbon-cutting ceremony is scheduled, concluded by a community aperitif. The initiative, accompanied by the motto 'solidarity well spent,' aims to become a reference point for supporting the vulnerabilities of the area.

### Detailed program

The program for the day has been structured to actively involve the community. The informational stands, open from 9:30 AM, will provide details on how the Emporium works and the methods of accessing the services. The project presentation, scheduled for 10 AM, will feature the participation of experienced volunteers from similar realities, such as the Emporium of Saronno, who will share their experiences and best practices.

The intervention of Monsignor Franco Agnesi, Vicar General of the Diocese, is expected at 10:30 AM. Monsignor Agnesi will bring his support and blessing to the initiative, emphasizing the importance of solidarity and mutual support in the community. The official ribbon-cutting ceremony, scheduled for 12 PM, will be a symbolic moment marking the official opening of the Solidarity Emporium.

### Community participation

The event is open to all citizens of Olgiate Olona and neighboring municipalities. The community aperitif, scheduled for 12:30 PM, will be an opportunity to socialize and get to know the local entities involved in the project better. The Solidarity Emporium aims to be a reference point for those in need of food and social support, offering not only necessities but also concrete and human support.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Lombardy, the Solidarity Emporium represents a precious resource. Many cross-border workers face economic and social challenges, and access to support services like this can make a difference. The Emporium offers not only food but also a sense of community and support, fundamental elements for those living in a situation of vulnerability.

### Conclusion

The inauguration of the Solidarity Emporium of Olgiate Olona is an important step towards a more supportive and inclusive community. The event on May 9 will be an opportunity to celebrate this new initiative and reflect on the importance of solidarity and mutual support. Everyone is invited to participate and discover how they can contribute to or benefit from this project. The Solidarity Emporium in Olgiate Olona is not just a food distribution point, but a true hub of aggregation and support for the community. Its inauguration represents an opportunity to strengthen social bonds and offer concrete help to those in need. The initiative is the result of collaboration between various local entities, which have decided to join forces to create a solidarity project that can have a lasting impact.

### Local Collaborations

The participation of numerous local entities is a sign of the strong social cohesion that characterizes Olgiate Olona and the surrounding municipalities. The Mensa del Padre Nostro and the CAV of Castellanza, the Amicizia Group of Gorla Minore, Icore of Marnate, Il pane di san Martino of Fagnano and Gorla Maggiore, together with the Sichem and Pachamama communities and the Promozione e Lavoro cooperative, represent an example of how collaboration can lead to significant results. These organizations have long experience in supporting vulnerabilities and will be fundamental to the success of the Emporium.

### Services Offered

The Solidarity Emporium will not limit itself to food distribution. Among the services offered will also be social counseling, psychological support, and aggregation activities. This holistic approach aims to provide comprehensive help to those in difficulty, addressing not only immediate needs but also the root causes of vulnerabilities. The goal is to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where people can feel supported and valued.

### Project Sustainability

To ensure the sustainability of the project, the Solidarity Emporium relies on voluntary contributions and donations. The community is invited to actively participate, both as donors and volunteers. Every contribution, large or small, is precious and helps keep this solidarity project alive. Furthermore, the Emporium will collaborate with other local organizations to optimize resources and maximize the positive impact on the community.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Lombardy, the Solidarity Emporium represents a valuable resource. Many cross-border workers face economic and social challenges, and access to support services like this can make a difference. The Emporium offers not only food but also a sense of community and support, elements fundamental for those living in a situation of vulnerability. Moreover, the geographical proximity between Olgiate Olona and Ticino facilitates access to services for cross-border workers, who can thus benefit from concrete and nearby support.

### Conclusion

The Solidarity Emporium in Olgiate Olona is an ambitious and necessary project that responds to a real need in the community. Its inauguration on May 9, 2026, will be an opportunity to celebrate solidarity and mutual support, values fundamental for a fairer and more inclusive society. Everyone is invited to participate and contribute, because every small gesture can make a difference.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). To participate in the inauguration of the Solidarity Emporium in Olgiate Olona, you can simply show up at the parish of San Giuseppe al Buon Gesù on the day of the event. No reservation is required, but it is advisable to arrive a bit early to have access to all the information stands and to participate in the project presentation.

### How to Contribute

If you wish to contribute to the project, there are several ways to do so. You can donate food items or other essential products directly to the Emporium. Additionally, you can offer your time as a volunteer, participating in distribution and support activities. Every contribution is valuable and helps to keep this solidarity project alive.

### Useful Contacts

For further information about the Solidarity Emporium and the ways to participate, you can contact the Decanato Valle Olona or the local entities involved in the project. Contacts will be available during the event and on the official channels of the participating organizations.

### Conclusion

The inauguration of the Solidarity Emporium in Olgiate Olona is an important step towards a more supportive and inclusive community. Participating in this event means supporting a project aimed at making a difference in the lives of many people. Do not miss the opportunity to be part of this initiative and to contribute to creating a better future for everyone.

item.title: 'God as my Witness' wins OtherMovie Lugano 2026
item.description: The winning film of the OtherMovie Lugano 2026 festival tackles the delicate issue of sexual abuse with over 1,200 attendees and three sold-out screenings
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- OtherMovie Lugano 2026 registers over 1,200 attendees
- 'God as my Witness' wins the festival
- Three sold-out events confirm the festival's strength

## Key facts
- **What**: OtherMovie Lugano 2026 Winner
- When: 17-25 April 2026
- Where: Lugano
- Who: Director Lindsay Q. Pitre
- Attendance: 1,200

The 2026 edition of OtherMovie Lugano concluded with great success, registering over 1,200 attendees at the events and three sold-out screenings. The winning film, 'God as my Witness – Lo giuro a Dio', tackled the delicate issue of sexual abuse, one of the central themes of the event. The fifteenth edition of the festival, held from 17 to 25 April, focused on the theme of change. The film by American director Lindsay Q. Pitre was awarded for its incisive investigation into the testimonies of victims and the institutional responsibilities within the Catholic Archdiocese of New Orleans. The jury, chaired by Matteo Balsamo, praised the film for its stylistic solidity, effective editing, and careful photography, calling it a universal appeal for justice.

In the L’Incontro section, dedicated to international feature films, 'Mamma Yamuna' by Vito Robbiani and 'Brides' by Nadia Fall were also awarded. The former addressed the issue of environmental pollution, while the latter told the story of two Muslim teenagers fleeing difficult family contexts. Among the short films, 'Tenére, tenére, tenére… fino alla fine' by Edoardo Nerboni won in the OtherSwiss Short category for the depth of its narrative and emotional intensity. The award for the best international short film went to the Spanish 'Tribù' by Carlos Gomez Trigo, while the award for best direction was given to 'Phosgene Tears to Old Kassandra' by Alessandra Pescetta, made with the help of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Meloni's labour decree: fair wage and end to pirate contracts
item.description: Green light for the labour decree with fair wage and incentives for hiring. Meloni: pact with businesses and unions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Green light for the fair wage labor decree
- Incentives for hiring and stop to pirate contracts
- Meloni: pact with businesses and unions

## Key facts
- **What**: Fair wage labor decree and incentives
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Italian government, businesses, unions
- Amount: Not yet specified

The labor decree has received the green light from the council of ministers. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni announced the measure at a press conference, along with Undersecretary Alfredo Mantovano and the ministers of Labor, Marina Calderone, and Equal Opportunities, Eugenia Roccella. The decree introduces the so-called 'fair wage,' defined as the overall economic treatment established by national collective contracts signed by the most representative employers' and workers' organizations at the national level. This measure aims to counter pirate contracts and guarantee fair economic treatment for workers. The prime minister emphasized the importance of an alliance with intermediary bodies, trade unions, and employers' associations, ensuring that the government is open to collecting improvement proposals. Minister Calderone reiterated the government's choice to support high-quality national collective bargaining.

### Main novelties
The decree introduces several novelties, including the fair wage and changes to the rule on contractual renewals. The fair wage is defined as the overall economic treatment determined by the most representative national collective contracts. This measure excludes those who sign pirate contracts or underpay workers from accessing benefits. Furthermore, the rule on contractual renewals has been modified to give greater flexibility to the signatory parties in regulating wage increases and any one-time amounts.

The labor decree represents a starting point for an alliance between the government, businesses, and unions, with the aim of guaranteeing a fairer and more sustainable labor market. The measures introduced aim to counter digital exploitation and encourage hiring, offering fair economic treatment to workers.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
In Ticino, for example, the average salary for a worker in the tertiary sector is approximately 6,500 CHF per month. With the introduction of the fair wage, companies operating in Italy and employing cross-border workers from Ticino will have to comply with Italian national collective contracts, which provide a minimum economic treatment of 1,500 euros per month for a full-time worker. This means that a cross-border worker from Ticino who moves to Italy to work will have to receive at least 1,500 euros, regardless of their previous salary in Switzerland.

### Regulations and dates
The labor decree will come into force on January 1, 2027. Companies will have until June 30, 2027, to comply with the new regulations. Penalties for those who do not respect the fair wage will range from 5,000 to 50,000 euros, depending on the severity of the infringement.

### Operational checklist
1. Contract verification: Check that existing contracts comply with the most representative national collective contracts.
2. Wage adjustment: Adjust workers' wages based on the new regulations.
3. Training: Train human resources managers on the new regulations.
4. Monitoring: Continuously monitor compliance with the new regulations.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Before the introduction of the decree, an Italian company hiring cross-border workers from Ticino could offer salaries lower than those provided by national collective contracts. With the new decree, these companies will have to comply with the new standards, guaranteeing fair economic treatment to workers. This could lead to an increase in labor costs for companies, but also to greater stability and satisfaction for workers.

> "The labor decree represents an important step towards a fairer and more sustainable labor market," declared Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

The labor decree represents a starting point for an alliance between the government, businesses, and unions, with the aim of guaranteeing a fairer and more sustainable labor market. The measures introduced aim to counter digital exploitation and encourage hiring, offering fair economic treatment to workers. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The labor decree introduces several measures that can have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland. In particular, the introduction of the **fair salary** can ensure more equitable economic treatment for workers, including cross-border commuters. This provision, which came into effect on January 1, 2024, aims to combat pirate contracts and ensure that workers receive adequate economic treatment based on the most representative national collective contracts.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the labor decree, cross-border workers could be subject to pirate contracts that did not guarantee adequate economic treatment. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Lugano who worked in Italy with a pirate contract could receive a salary 20-30% lower than an Italian colleague with a regular contract. With the new provision, workers will be entitled to a fair salary and incentives for hiring. This can have a positive impact on the quality of life of cross-border workers and their ability to plan for the future.

### Concrete scenarios

Suppose a cross-border worker who works in Italy and resides in Switzerland has a pirate contract. With the introduction of the labor decree, this worker will be entitled to a fair salary based on the most representative national collective contracts. For example, a construction worker residing in Mendrisio who worked in Italy with a pirate contract of 1,200 euros per month could now see their salary increase to 1,500-1,600 euros, in line with the collective contracts of the sector. Furthermore, the employer who signs pirate contracts will not be entitled to public work incentives, such as contributions for professional training or tax benefits.

📊 Practical example:
- Before the decree: Monthly salary of 1,200 euros for a cross-border construction worker.
- After the decree: Monthly salary of 1,500-1,600 euros, in line with collective contracts.

### Procedure for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who wish to benefit from the new measures introduced by the labor decree must ensure that they have a contract in line with the most representative national collective contracts. Here is an operational checklist:

1. Contract verification: Check that the employment contract is in line with national collective contracts.
2. Consultation: In case of doubt, consult a labor expert or a union to obtain detailed information and assistance.
3. Documentation: Keep all documentation relating to the contract and communications with the employer.
4. Reporting: In case of irregularities, report the situation to the competent authorities or unions.

💡 Practical advice:
> "If your contract is not in line with the collective contracts, immediately contact a union or a labor expert for assistance."

### Comparison of practical scenarios

Scenario 1: Compliant contract
- A cross-border worker residing in Chiasso who works in Italy with a contract in line with the collective contracts receives a monthly salary of 1,800 euros.
- Benefits from all public work incentives, such as contributions for professional training.

Scenario 2: Non-compliant contract
- A cross-border worker residing in Bellinzona who works in Italy with a pirate contract receives a monthly salary of 1,300 euros.
- Does not benefit from public work incentives and risks sanctions.

⚠️ Warning:
Employers who continue to use pirate contracts may be subject to administrative and criminal sanctions, with fines that can reach up to 10,000 euros.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

To benefit from the new measures introduced by the labor decree of January 1, 2024, cross-border workers must follow some concrete procedures. First, it is important to verify that their employment contract complies with the most representative national collective labor agreements, such as the CCNL for employees of professional firms or that for commerce workers. If not, it is advisable to contact the employer to request an adjustment of the contract.

### Steps to follow

1. **Check your employment contract**
 - Ensure that the salary complies with the contractual minimums. For example, a construction worker in Lugano should earn at least CHF 3,900 per month for full-time work.
 - Make sure that the annual leave is at least 20 working days, as provided for by the construction CCNL.

2. Contact the employer to request a contract adjustment, if necessary
 - Send a formal request via email or registered letter, citing the specific regulations.
 - Example of a request: 'Based on Article 3 of the construction CCNL, I request an adjustment of my salary to CHF 3,900 per month.'

3. Consult a labor expert or a union to obtain detailed information and assistance
 - Unions such as USS or ASI can provide legal and contractual support.
 - Example: a cross-border worker from Chiasso can turn to USS for free advice.

4. Monitor official communications from the government and unions to stay updated on new measures and available benefits
 - Subscribe to the newsletters of the Ticino Department of Labor and regularly consult the Frontalieri Ticino website.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who wish to verify the compliance of their employment contract, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontalieri Ticino website can be used. This tool can help determine whether their economic treatment complies with the most representative national collective labor agreements and identify any discrepancies.

📊 Practical example:
- A cross-border worker from Mendrisio with a monthly salary of CHF 3,500 discovers, through the calculator, that their salary is below the contractual minimum of CHF 3,700 for their sector. They can then request an adjustment from the employer.

### Operational checklist

- [ ] Verify the CCNL applicable to your sector
- [ ] Check that the salary complies with the contractual minimums
- [ ] Verify the number of annual leave days provided
- [ ] Request a contractual adjustment, if necessary
- [ ] Consult an expert or a union for assistance

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker from Campione d'Italia with a contract not compliant with the construction CCNL can request an increase from CHF 3,500 to CHF 3,900 per month.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker from Bellinzona with a contract compliant with the commerce CCNL does not require any action, but can still verify other benefits provided by the labor decree.

### Final CTA

For further information and assistance, cross-border workers can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontalieri Ticino website. This tool can help verify the compliance of their employment contract and identify any discrepancies.

⚠️ Important note: The procedures must be followed by June 30, 2024 to benefit from the new measures.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/29/leggi-notizia/articolo/lavoro-via-libera-a-decreto-meloni-patto-con-imprese-e-sindacati-niente-ai-pirati.html)*

item.title: Heartfelt trip to Soncino to support Saronno Point
item.description: A day of history, art, and solidarity to fund Saronno Point's activities for cancer patients.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Heartfelt trip to Soncino to support Saronno Point
- Participation fee: 70 euros, deposit of 35 euros
- Registrations at Saronno Point's headquarters
- Concrete example: in 2023, the trip raised 5,000 euros for home care projects

## Key facts
- **What**: Heartfelt trip to Soncino
- When: Sunday, May 17, 2026
- Where: Soncino, province of Cremona
- Who: Saronno Point Odv
- Fee: 70 euros per person, deposit of 35 euros
- Registration deadline: Thursday and Saturday from 15:00 to 18:00
- Operational checklist: 
 - Payment of 35 euro deposit
 - Completion of registration form
 - Check availability of seats (max 50 participants)

A day between history, art, and solidarity to support those facing illness. Saronno Point Odv is organizing a new “Heartfelt Trip” for Sunday, May 17, an initiative open to the public with destination Soncino, in the province of Cremona, one of the most fascinating villages in Lombardy. The trip represents an opportunity to combine the recreational moment with the solidarity one, contributing directly to the activities of the organization.

Discovering Soncino
The chosen destination is Soncino, known for its Sforza castle and well-preserved medieval center. The day will allow participants to visit the village and spend time together, also enhancing the cultural heritage of the territory. For residents of Ticino, this trip represents an opportunity to explore a nearby but often overlooked destination, with a cost accessible compared to longer trips. For example, a similar trip to Lugano could cost up to 100 euros per person, while the trip to Soncino offers a similar experience at a lower price.

Information and registrations
Participation costs 70 euros per person, with a deposit of 35 euros at the time of registration. Registrations are made at the headquarters of Saronno Point, in via San Giuseppe 41 in Saronno, on Thursday and Saturday from 15:00 to 18:00. For information, the number 338 63774481 is available. It is important to note that, according to Italian regulations, volunteer organizations like Saronno Point Odv must respect precise guidelines for the transparency of the funds raised. In 2023, for example, the organization published a detailed report showing that 85% of the funds raised were used for home care projects.

⚠️ Attention: Seats are limited to 50 participants, so it is advisable to register in advance. Furthermore, for residents in Switzerland, it is important to verify the tax regulations regarding money transfers to Italy. According to Swiss laws, transfers exceeding 10,000 CHF per year must be declared, but for lower amounts there are no specific obligations.

💡 Tip: For those who wish for a similar experience but closer to Ticino, it might be interesting to consider a trip to Locarno or Bellinzona, which offer equally rich historical and cultural heritage. However, the trip to Soncino represents a unique opportunity to support a solidarity cause and discover a new destination. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The 'Gita del cuore' represents a significant opportunity for cross-border workers employed in Ticino, offering them the chance to participate in solidarity initiatives without having to undertake long journeys. According to data from the Federal Statistical Office, in 2023 there were approximately 68,000 cross-border workers in Ticino, primarily from Varese, Como, and Novara. This initiative allows for combining a cultural experience with a concrete contribution to support activities for cancer patients and their families, such as the accompaniment services and complementary therapies offered by Saronno Point.

### Comparison with other initiatives
Compared to other organized outings, this one stands out for its dual objective: offering a high-quality tourist and cultural experience while supporting an important social cause. The choice of Soncino as the destination adds extra value, allowing participants to discover a historic village of great charm. Unlike other initiatives, such as company outings that often limit themselves to recreational events, the 'Gita del cuore' integrates an element of solidarity, making it more meaningful.

### Practical scenarios
Cross-border workers who wish to participate can plan their day to balance work commitments. For example, a cross-border worker employed in Lugano could leave early in the morning and return in the afternoon, taking advantage of rail connections such as the regional train that connects Lugano to Varese in about an hour and a half. The outing is an opportunity to spend a different day, away from the daily routine, while contributing to a charitable cause at the same time. Participation is open to all, making the initiative inclusive and accessible.

### Operational checklist
- **Travel planning**: Check train and bus schedules to optimize travel time.
- Ticket cost: The price of the ticket for the outing is 50 CHF, which includes transportation, guided tour, and lunch.
- Required documentation: Bring a valid ID and, if necessary, the residence permit.
- Solidarity contribution: Prepare an additional donation to support Saronno Point, if desired.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Cross-border worker from Varese: Can leave at 7:00 AM and return at 6:00 PM, with a total transport cost of approximately 30 CHF.
- Cross-border worker from Como: Can leave at 6:30 AM and return at 5:30 PM, with a total transport cost of approximately 40 CHF.
- Cross-border worker from Novara: Can leave at 6:00 AM and return at 6:30 PM, with a total transport cost of approximately 50 CHF.

### Regulations and benefits
Cross-border workers employed in Ticino benefit from certain tax advantages, such as the deduction of transportation expenses. According to Swiss regulations, transportation expenses can be deducted up to a maximum of 3,000 CHF per year. Additionally, cross-border workers can take advantage of discounts on train tickets, such as the reduced-rate monthly pass for commuters.

> “The 'Gita del cuore' is an initiative that combines culture and solidarity, offering cross-border workers a unique opportunity to contribute to an important cause.”

### Conclusion
The 'Gita del cuore' to Soncino represents a precious opportunity for cross-border workers employed in Ticino, allowing them to participate in a solidarity initiative without having to undertake long journeys. With careful planning and the use of available benefits, cross-border workers can spend a meaningful day while contributing to an important social cause.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate
To participate in the "Gita del cuore" to Soncino, follow these simple steps:

1. **Registration**: Visit the Saronno Point office at via San Giuseppe 41 in Saronno on Thursdays and Saturdays from 3 PM to 6 PM. Cross-border workers from Ticino, such as those from Mendrisio or Chiasso, can plan their visit based on their work hours. For example, someone working in Lugano and returning home in the afternoon can easily stop by Saronno on their way back.

2. Payment: Pay a deposit of 35 euros at the time of registration and the remaining 35 euros later. Payment can be made in cash or by credit card. For cross-border workers, it's useful to know that the euro-Swiss franc exchange rate is currently favorable, with a rate of approximately 1.05 CHF for 1 EUR, making the trip more affordable.

3. Information: Contact the number 338 63774481 for more details. You can also send an email to info@saronnopoint.it to request specific information.

### Required Documents
No specific documents are required for participation, but it is advisable to bring a copy of the payment receipt and, if available, a Saronno Point membership card. For cross-border workers, it is useful to have a valid ID document, especially if traveling in a group and needing to prove group membership.

### Deadlines
Registrations are open until all spots are filled. It is advisable to book in advance to secure your participation. For example, for a similar trip organized last year, all spots were filled in less than two weeks. For cross-border workers, it is important to consider cross-border work regulations, such as EU Regulation 883/2004, which ensures equal treatment in social security matters.

### Useful Tools
To plan the day effectively, cross-border workers can use the [travel calculator](nav:transport) to estimate travel times and organize their itinerary. For example, a cross-border worker from Mendrisio takes approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes to reach Saronno by car, while a colleague from Chiasso takes about 1 hour and 15 minutes. Additionally, the [cost comparator](nav:cost-of-living) can be useful for evaluating the overall expenses of the trip. For example, the cost of fuel for a round trip from Lugano to Saronno is approximately 20 CHF, while the highway toll is approximately 10 CHF.

### Operational Checklist
- Booking: Contact Saronno Point to reserve your spot.
- Payment: Pay the deposit of 35 euros.
- Documents: Bring the payment receipt and an ID document.
- Planning: Use the travel calculator to organize your itinerary.
- Expenses: Evaluate the overall costs using the cost comparator.

### Practical Scenario Comparisons
For a cross-border worker from Mendrisio who works in Lugano, the trip to Soncino can be an opportunity to spend a day differently. With a total cost of 70 euros, which includes travel and the contribution to the cause, the trip is affordable. Additionally, thanks to EU regulations, the cross-border worker can benefit from the same protections as Italian workers, such as health insurance and pension.

### Conclusion
The "Gita del cuore" to Soncino is an initiative that combines culture, solidarity, and fun. Cross-border workers who wish to participate can do so easily by following the provided instructions and contributing to an important cause. For more information and to register, visit the Saronno Point website or contact the indicated number.

> "Solidarity knows no borders, and this trip is a concrete example" - A participant from the previous edition.

For cross-border workers from Ticino, participating in the trip also represents an opportunity to better know the Italian territory and create bonds with the local community.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/una-gita-del-cuore-alla-scoperta-di-soncino-per-sostenere-le-attivita-di-saronno-point/2560099/)*

item.title: China blocks Manus acquisition: here's why
item.description: Beijing halts Meta's }; billion purchase, fearing loss of strategic technology
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- China blocks Meta's acquisition of Manus
- Operation worth 2 billion US dollars
- Manus is an autonomous artificial intelligence agent

## Key facts
- **What**: Blocking of Manus acquisition
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: China
- Who: National Development and Reform Commission of Beijing
- Amount: 2 billion US dollars

The action by Chinese authorities seems to be linked to what is known as 'Singapore Washing,' the trend of tech startups paving the way for foreign acquisitions or investments by moving their legal headquarters abroad. Yesterday, 08:33 Yesterday, 08:42 02:27 News broadcast at 07:00 on 28.04.2026: China's block on Meta's acquisition of the Manus platform RSI Info 28.04.2026, 07:00 Reuters Audio content By: Lorenzo Lamperti, RSI collaborator from Taiwan 'Don't sell the young crops.' In recent weeks, some Chinese analysts had used this expression to comment on Meta's acquisition of Manus. Translated: do not give away too early something that, if left to mature, could become much more valuable. This is the perspective behind China's decisive intervention in the most talked-about deal of recent months, at least in the field of artificial intelligence. The National Development and Reform Commission of Beijing announced that it will 'prohibit foreign investments in the acquisition of the Manus project' and 'require the parties involved to withdraw the transaction.' A few hours later, Meta confirmed the reversal of the acquisition announced in December, in a deal worth over two billion US dollars.

### What is Manus?
Manus is owned by Butterfly Effect, a tech startup whose legal headquarters has been moved to Singapore. It is one of the most promising artificial intelligence systems in the world: Manus is not a 'simple' chatbot like ChatGPT or the Chinese Deepseek, but what is defined as an 'autonomous agent.' The platform is capable of managing complex activities such as building financial analysis tools, preparing advanced reports or business presentations, developing codes. All this in complete autonomy. Even the name in Latin, which means 'hand,' underscores the credentials of an 'artificial intelligence that acts' and that 'does not work with the user,' but 'for the user.'

The acquisition of Manus by Meta represents a significant case with profound implications. Meta, the company behind Facebook and Instagram, has long sought to expand its presence in the artificial intelligence market. With the purchase of Manus, the US giant led by Mark Zuckerberg was expected to give a turn to its business, radically expanding services for users and businesses. However, at the end of March, the two co-founders of Manus, Xiao Hong and Ji Yichao, were summoned to Beijing. Both would have been prevented from leaving the national territory during the review process of the deal, culminating in a report that speaks of a 'conspiratorial attempt' aimed at 'depriving China of an advanced technological base in artificial intelligence.' Hence the order to block the deal, with the request to withdraw the transaction.

The affair has several ramifications and reminded some of what happened in the autumn of 2020, when Beijing blocked at the last minute the stock market listing of Ant Group, the fintech arm of the digital giant Alibaba. In that case, it was the beginning of the campaign to rectify the Big Tech that led to a strategic reorientation of the activities of the Chinese giants. Now, China seems to want to secure also a sector that is becoming increasingly crucial, such as that of artificial intelligence, among the main ingredients of the menu of the contest with the United States. ### Implications for the tech market
The blocking of Manus' acquisition by China has significant implications for the global tech market. Beijing's decision to ban foreign investments in strategic sectors like artificial intelligence signals a shift in Chinese economic policy. This could influence the expansion strategies of major Western tech companies, which may need to revise their acquisition plans in China.

### Impact on foreign investments
China is becoming increasingly protective of its advanced technologies. The phenomenon of 'Singapore Washing,' where companies move their legal headquarters abroad to facilitate foreign acquisitions or investments, is seen as a systemic risk. This could lead to greater regulation and control over foreign investments in critical sectors, such as artificial intelligence and fintech.

### Consequences for Chinese startups
Chinese startups seeking to internationalize in strategic sectors will need to coordinate with political authorities. China is preparing stricter regulations to require government approval for U.S. investments in tech startups. This could slow down the internationalization process and make it harder for Chinese startups to attract foreign investments.

### Comparison with similar situations
The Manus case reminds us of Ant Group, when Beijing blocked the IPO of Alibaba's fintech arm. At the time, it marked the beginning of a campaign to rectify Chinese Big Tech. Now, China seems to want to apply the same level of control to the artificial intelligence sector, which is at the center of tensions with the United States.

### Future scenarios
If China continues to strengthen control over critical technologies, we could see an increase in commercial tensions with the United States. This could lead to a greater fragmentation of the global tech market, with significant consequences for companies and investors.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do for tech companies
Tech companies operating in China or planning to invest in the country must be aware of the new rules and restrictions imposed by the Chinese government. It is crucial to closely monitor regulatory developments and consult legal experts to fully understand the implications of the new laws.

### Concrete steps for startups
Startups looking to internationalize must carefully evaluate the sectors they operate in and ensure they comply with local regulations. It is important to coordinate with political authorities and obtain the necessary government approvals before proceeding with foreign acquisitions or investments.

### Use support tools
To better understand the implications of the new regulations and plan their expansion strategies, companies can use tools such as the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare). These tools can help evaluate the available options and make informed decisions.

### Conclusion
The blocking of Manus' acquisition by China represents an important signal for the global tech market. Companies and startups must be ready to adapt to the new regulations and coordinate with political authorities to avoid legal and financial problems. Using support tools and consulting legal experts can make a difference in the success of their expansion strategies.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3698082)*

item.title: Agesp: Waste collection schedule for May 1st 2026
item.description: Changes to urban waste collection services in Busto Arsizio, Fagnano Olona and Venegono Superiore for Labor Day.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Agesp administrative offices are closed on May 1st.
- Waste collection in Busto Arsizio will take place as scheduled.
- The service is suspended in Fagnano Olona and Venegono Superiore.
- Waste collection will be recovered on May 2nd in the affected areas.

## Key facts
- What: Reorganization of urban hygiene services
- When: Friday, May 1st, 2026
- Where: Busto Arsizio, Fagnano Olona, Venegono Superiore
- Who: Agesp Spa Group and Agesp Ambiente
- Status: Administrative offices closed

In view of the Labor Day holiday on May 1st, 2026, the Agesp Spa and Agesp Ambiente group has officially announced the operational plan for urban hygiene services. The schedule provides for a clear differentiation between the various municipalities managed, affecting the daily waste management for citizens and cross-border commuters who transit through or reside in these areas. In Busto Arsizio, the municipality will maintain the continuity of the door-to-door collection service. Despite the holiday, sanitation workers will operate according to standard procedures without any interruption. This decision aims to ensure urban decorum in the most populous town, avoiding waste accumulation that could result from a forced day of suspension. Conversely, the situation appears different for the neighboring towns of Fagnano Olona and Venegono Superiore. In these two municipalities, the door-to-door collection scheduled for Friday, May 1st, has been officially suspended. The calendar will therefore undergo a planned shift: waste collection operations will be recovered on Saturday, May 2nd, 2026, following the usual timings provided by standard municipal calendars. It is necessary for residents to plan their waste disposal taking into account this twenty-four-hour delay to avoid service disruptions. The organization emphasizes that such rescheduling is a consolidated practice for managing mid-week holidays, balancing the needs of territory maintenance with the rights of workers in the sector. The environmental services sector remains sensitive to these variations, as the management of the waste cycle requires constant logistical coordination between the municipalities and the managing body, especially in a high-density residential context like the Altomilanese area. For those who need further details on the [collection calendar](nav:calendar), it is advisable to consult the official portals before the holiday. ### Operational Analysis of Closures

In addition to home collection, the entire administrative and public service apparatus of the Agesp group will remain inactive on the public holiday of May 1st. This total closure affects not only the management offices but also direct points of contact with the public that are fundamental for resolving bureaucratic procedures related to waste management and urban mobility. Specifically, the Tarip Office on via Pepe, the Parking Sector offices located on via Mazzini, the Territory Services at the Marzoli Mills, and the Environmental Hygiene Office on via Canale will be inaccessible. The closure also extends to crucial infrastructures for the territory. The four municipal pharmacies managed by the Agesp group, the Crematorium Temple on via per Samarate, and the Multiraccolta Center on via Tosi in Busto Arsizio will close their shutters for the entire duration of the holiday. A similar closure scenario applies to the Multiraccolta Centers in Fagnano Olona on via Nobile and in Venegono Superiore on via Ferrarin. For a cross-border worker or a citizen who had planned to dispose of bulky waste or special waste at the collection centers, it is essential to anticipate or postpone the operation. Going to the facilities on May 1st would result in a wasted trip, as access will not be allowed at any of the mentioned locations. This reorganization, although limited to the specific day, highlights the importance of consulting the [municipal services](nav:municipalities) to avoid logistical complications. There are no exceptions communicated for the mentioned facilities; therefore, the resumption of all administrative activities and collection centers will take place regularly on the following working day, i.e., Saturday, May 2, 2026. It is advisable to plan domestic or office disposal activities keeping in mind that the blockage of the collection centers follows a criterion of uniformity throughout the basin managed by Agesp, without distinctions between the mentioned municipalities. This planning ensures that staff can benefit from the holiday, while maintaining essential collection services where provided.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Waste Disposal and Management Procedures

To optimize waste management during a bank holiday or festive day, it is useful to follow some practical guidelines based on the provisions provided by Agesp. For residents of Busto Arsizio, no changes are required in their usual behavior: bins and bags must be displayed at the times specified by the standard calendar of their neighborhood. The regularity of the service ensures that there are no anomalies in the disposal. For those residing or working in Fagnano Olona and Venegono Superiore, the correct procedure is not to display waste on Friday, May 1st. The suspension of the service requires that the deposit be made directly in the morning or evening of Saturday, May 2nd, in accordance with the type of waste provided for that specific day of the calendar. Failure to comply with these instructions may result in waste remaining in the street, with potential aesthetic or hygienic issues. Those who need specific information can use digital consultation tools to verify their [personalized collection calendar](nav:calendar). It is always advisable to consult the official communications of the authority for any last-minute updates. For citizens who need to calculate the cost of services or monitor spending related to waste management and other utilities, it is possible to use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the fiscal guidance portals present on our site, useful for having an overview of fixed and variable costs in Ticino and neighboring areas. Conscious waste management, especially in areas with strong work exchange, is an element that contributes to the quality of daily life. We remind you that, although the information provided covers the municipalities of Busto Arsizio, Fagnano Olona, and Venegono Superiore, any specific doubts about particular types of waste (such as WEEE or hazardous materials) must be resolved by directly contacting the Agesp offices on working days prior to the holiday. Proper planning prevents fines or disservices related to the non-collection of waste during periods of national holidays.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/primo-maggio-cosa-cambia-e-cosa-no-nella-raccolta-rifiuti-a-busto-arsizio-fagnano-olona-e-venegono-superiore/2560639/)*

item.title: Seized warehouse in Como: investigation into abandoned cars
item.description: Como's local police have seized a body shop warehouse for environmental violations, discovering abandoned waste and irregularities in the temporary storage.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Body shop warehouse seized in Como for environmental violations
- Investigations started from car parts abandoned in public areas
- Over 80 fines issued for incorrect waste disposal

## Key facts
- **What**: Seizure of a body shop warehouse for environmental violations
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Como
- Who: Local police of Como
- Amount: Over 80 fines issued for incorrect waste disposal
- Who: Four cases of driving license suspension (four to six months) and fines between 1,000 and 3,000 euros
- Who: Seizure of a truck used for illegal waste transport

The action against environmental crimes in the Larian territory has recorded a new decisive intervention. Through integrated monitoring between surveillance cameras, checks by environmental inspectors and voluntary ecological guards, the local police of Como identified and fined the responsible parties for an uncontrolled waste deposit. The origin of the investigation stemmed from the discovery of mechanical components and spare parts of vehicles abandoned in public areas. The investigations conducted by the agents allowed to initially trace the owner of the vehicle and subsequently the professional in charge of the repair.

The investigative results imposed an immediate verification at the activity's headquarters to ascertain the conformity of the waste storage and disposal procedures. The inspection confirmed the non-compliance with the current regulations regarding temporary storage. The operators noted the presence of a disorderly accumulation of various items within the company perimeter. Given the violations of the Environmental Code (Legislative Decree 152/2006) and the configuration of criminal charges against the company's representatives, the external area was seized to prevent the worsening of environmental damage.

### Sanctions activity balance
Since the beginning of 2026, the surveillance activity has produced significant results in territorial control. Over 80 fines issued for incorrect disposal and violations of the municipal regulation. Four cases of driving license suspension (four to six months) and fines between 1,000 and 3,000 euros, in application of the Terra dei Fuochi Decree. Seizure of a truck used for illegal waste transport in the absence of the necessary authorizations.

The use of dedicated technological equipment and the planning of targeted services by the Specialist Unit for Environmental and Territorial Protection will remain pillars of the Command's strategy, which plans a further increase in controls in the coming weeks. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The recent crackdown on environmental crimes in the Como area has significant implications for cross-border workers who work or reside in the region. It is crucial to be aware of the current regulations regarding waste management and the penalties for violations. Cross-border workers in the vehicle repair sector must ensure they comply with waste storage and disposal procedures to avoid fines and seizures.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
Before these operations, the Como area had seen an increase in cases of illegal dumping and uncontrolled waste deposits. The surveillance activities conducted by the local police have led to a significant improvement in the situation, with a growing number of fines issued and seizures made. This demonstrates the local authorities' commitment to combating environmental crimes and protecting the environment.

### Concrete scenarios
For cross-border workers in the vehicle repair sector, it is important to follow the correct procedures for waste disposal. For example, it is necessary to separate recyclable materials from non-recyclable ones and dispose of them at authorized collection centers. Additionally, it is essential to stay updated on current regulations and any changes to environmental laws.

### Concrete procedures
To avoid fines and seizures, cross-border workers must follow these procedures:
1. Identify and separate waste according to its nature.
2. Dispose of waste at authorized collection centers.
3. Stay updated on current regulations regarding waste management.
4. Collaborate with local authorities to ensure compliance with environmental regulations.

### Site tools
For further information and to verify the compliance of waste management procedures, cross-border workers can use the [calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the penalties for violations of environmental regulations and verify the compliance of waste storage and disposal procedures.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers in the vehicle repair sector must adopt concrete measures to ensure compliance with waste management procedures. First and foremost, it is essential to identify and separate waste according to its nature, disposing of it at authorized collection centers. Additionally, it is important to stay up-to-date with current regulations and any changes made to environmental laws.

### Deadlines and procedures
Local authorities have planned an increase in inspections in the coming weeks. Therefore, cross-border workers must be ready to demonstrate compliance with waste management procedures. In case of violations, penalties can range from fines to the seizure of the affected areas.

### Site tools
To verify the compliance of waste management procedures and to calculate any penalties provided, cross-border workers can use the [calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to verify the compliance of waste storage and disposal procedures and to calculate the penalties provided for violations of environmental regulations.

### Final CTA
For further information and to verify the compliance of waste management procedures, cross-border workers can use the [calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the penalties provided for violations of environmental regulations and to verify the compliance of waste storage and disposal procedures.
